articles.title: Listen to F1 Nation's Australian GP review
articles.summary: Listen to F1 Nation's Australian GP review. George Russell got his 2026 Championship charge off to the perfect possible start with victory in Melbourne, but Kimi Antonelli ran him close and Ferrari were also in the mix.

articles.title: What time is the 2026 Chinese GP and how can I watch it?
articles.summary: What time is the 2026 Chinese GP and how can I watch it?. Here are all the timings – along with all the additional information you need – for the second Grand Prix weekend of the 2026 season from Shanghai International Circuit in China.

articles.title: 9 moments you missed at the Australian Grand Prix
articles.summary: 9 moments you missed at the Australian Grand Prix. Lego helmets, cricket collaborations and hectic home race events – here are the 2026 Australian Grand Prix off-track moments you might have missed.

articles.title: 6 Winners and 5 Losers from Australia
articles.summary: 6 Winners and 5 Losers from Australia. Lawrence Barretto picks out his winners and losers from the opening Grand Prix of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

articles.title: McLaren have ‘work ahead of us' as Stella identifies main objective
articles.summary: McLaren have ‘work ahead of us' as Stella identifies main objective. McLaren Team Principal Andrea Stella explained in more detail what his team need to do to catch up to Mercedes and Ferrari after being the third-fastest team in Melbourne.

articles.title: Verstappen to make Nurburgring 24 Hours debut
articles.summary: Verstappen to make Nurburgring 24 Hours debut. Max Verstappen has confirmed that he will make his debut at the Nurburgring 24 Hours in May with Mercedes, ticking off an event that has “been on my bucket list for a long time”.

articles.title: Vasseur explains Ferrari’s VSC strategy call in Australia
articles.summary: Vasseur explains Ferrari’s VSC strategy call in Australia. Fred Vasseur has outlined the thinking behind Ferrari’s decision not to pit under the Virtual Safety Car in the Australian Grand Prix, the team boss suggesting that there is plenty for the Scuderia to work on in order to close the performanc...

articles.title: Mercedes 'have a fight on our hands’ with Ferrari – Wolff
articles.summary: Mercedes 'have a fight on our hands’ with Ferrari – Wolff. Team Principal Toto Wolff has enthused that “Mercedes are back” after George Russell and Kimi Antonelli brought home a 1-2 for the squad in Australia, but conceded that they could be in for a tense competition with Ferrari.

articles.title: Aston Martin ‘motived’ and ‘determined’ to improve – Alonso
articles.summary: Aston Martin ‘motived’ and ‘determined’ to improve – Alonso. Fernando Alonso and Lance Stroll give their views on the Australian Grand Prix, and what positives Aston Martin can take from the opening race of the season.

articles.title: Could Ferrari have won in Australia with a different VSC call?
articles.summary: Could Ferrari have won in Australia with a different VSC call?. Mercedes secured a perfect 1-2 result in Melbourne, but could Ferrari have challenged for the win with a different decision?

articles.title: When is racing not racing? F1's new regulations deliver a split verdict
articles.summary: When is racing not racing? F1's new regulations deliver a split verdict. Chris Medland explores whether F1 racing is now too artificial, and whether drivers' opinions about driving satisfaction are worth considering.

articles.title: Aston Martin encouraged by Honda's progress on vibration issues
articles.summary: Aston Martin encouraged by Honda's progress on vibration issues. After Honda saw progress with its vibration issues, Aston Martin is confident it could have finished the Australian Grand Prix if it wasn’t managing its battery stock.

articles.title: Cadillac ‘way ahead of where we really should be’ after F1 debut
articles.summary: Cadillac ‘way ahead of where we really should be’ after F1 debut. Cadillac's long journey to the Formula 1 grid is finally complete, now the real work starts – but from a good foundation.

articles.title: No regrets from Ferrari drivers after staying out during VSC
articles.summary: No regrets from Ferrari drivers after staying out during VSC. It looked as if Ferrari might have thrown away a win when it didn't pit during a VSC, but its drivers say Mercedes were always going to be tough to catch in the Australian Grand Prix.

articles.title: Russell ‘lucky’ to not be worse off after poor start in the Australian GP
articles.summary: Russell ‘lucky’ to not be worse off after poor start in the Australian GP. George Russell had no battery and fell behind at the start of the Australian Grand Prix, but he was able to recover.

articles.title: Piastri caught out by unexpected power in pre-race crash
articles.summary: Piastri caught out by unexpected power in pre-race crash. While Oscar Piastri took a significant portion of the blame for the crash that ruled him out of his home race, he said a surge of power from his car's new power unit also played a part.

articles.title: Russell resists early Ferrari pressure to win Australian Grand Prix
articles.summary: Russell resists early Ferrari pressure to win Australian Grand Prix. George Russell led a Mercedes one-two finish in the first grand prix of the season as Ferrari followed the Silver Arrows home.

articles.title: How to watch the F1 Australian Grand Prix: Saturday
articles.summary: How to watch the F1 Australian Grand Prix: Saturday. Formula 1's 2026 season opener in Australia is set for tonight, for those of us in the USA. What sort of drama will the race from Melbourne deliver?

articles.title: Driver dissent surges after power unit struggles in Australian GP qualifying
articles.summary: Driver dissent surges after power unit struggles in Australian GP qualifying. Drivers are down – and not just on power – after sampling the latest breed of F1 cars in qualifying for the first time.

articles.title: Stroll permitted to start Australian Grand Prix despite not meeting 107% rule
articles.summary: Stroll permitted to start Australian Grand Prix despite not meeting 107% rule. Despite not setting a fast enough lap time to meet the 107% rule all weekend, Lance Stroll has been granted permission to race in the Australian Grand Prix.

articles.title: Verstappen to compete in 'bucket list' 24-hour race
articles.summary: Verstappen to compete in 'bucket list' 24-hour race. Four-time Formula 1 world champion Max Verstappen will tick off one of his "bucket list" events when he drives in May's Nurburgring 24-Hours endurance race.

articles.title: Was new-era F1 really racing or more like a computer game?
articles.summary: Was new-era F1 really racing or more like a computer game?. The opening race of F1's new era in Australia left the drivers with mixed feelings about what the sport had become, writes Andrew Benson.

articles.title: Russell 'imperious' and Bearman 'raced beautifully' - driver ratings
articles.summary: Russell 'imperious' and Bearman 'raced beautifully' - driver ratings. BBC Radio 5 Live F1 commentator Harry Benjamin rates how the drivers performed during the Australian Grand Prix.

articles.title: Brit rookie Lindblad 'living his dream' on F1 debut
articles.summary: Brit rookie Lindblad 'living his dream' on F1 debut. Racing Bulls' rookie Arvid Lindblad says he "showed people a bit of what I am here to do" after finishing eighth on his F1 debut at the Australian Grand Prix.

articles.title: New rules will lead to more 'drama' in F1 racing
articles.summary: New rules will lead to more 'drama' in F1 racing. The Chequered Flag podcast's Jennie Gow and Marc Priestly discuss how new F1 regulations will entice "casual fans" with more "drama" on the tracks.

articles.title: Mercedes 'hit ground running' with new F1 car regulations
articles.summary: Mercedes 'hit ground running' with new F1 car regulations. On the latest episode of The Chequered Flag Podcast, former McLaren mechanic Marc Priestly credits Mercedes for the way they have managed to "hit the ground running" on integrating new F1 car regulations.

articles.title: How to follow Chinese Grand Prix on the BBC
articles.summary: How to follow Chinese Grand Prix on the BBC. All the key BBC coverage details for round two of the Formula 1 season at the Chinese Grand Prix in Shanghai, which takes place from 13-15 March.

articles.title: F1: Chequered Flag
articles.summary: F1: Chequered Flag. Harry, Jennie and Marc look back at the race from Melbourne

articles.title: Russell wins first race of new F1 era in Australia
articles.summary: Russell wins first race of new F1 era in Australia. George Russell takes a comfortable victory in the Australian Grand Prix at the start of a new F1 era after an early scrap with Charles Leclerc.

articles.title: Andrew Benson Q&A: Send us your questions
articles.summary: Andrew Benson Q&A: Send us your questions. Our F1 correspondent Andrew Benson is doing a weekly Q&A this season, so get your questions in now.
